---
mode: agent
description: Make any website fully discoverable and well-described for search crawlers and AI agents — metadata, Open Graph/Twitter cards, JSON-LD, favicons, web app manifest, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, and llms.txt.
---

# Optimize this site for search crawlers and AI agents

Add everything a website needs to be **well indexed by search engines** and **well
understood by AI agents / LLMs**. Deliver a complete, verified set of discovery
metadata and machine-readable files. Implement changes — don't just recommend.

## 1. Discover the site first (don't assume)

Before editing, establish these facts and confirm with me only if ambiguous:

- **Build model:** Is the site static HTML, generated by a build tool / static-site
  generator / templating layer, or a SPA framework? If pages are generated from a
  template or partial, make the changes **in the generator/shared layout** so they
  survive rebuilds — never only in generated output.
- **Canonical origin & base path:** the production URL (scheme + host, and any
  sub-path). All canonical/Open Graph/JSON-LD URLs must be **absolute** against it.
  If you can't find it, ask me for it.
- **Deployed output directory:** which folder actually ships. Every asset you
  reference (icons, social image, manifest, robots, sitemap, llms.txt) must live
  **inside that published directory**.
- **Existing brand imagery:** locate a logo / cover / hero image to derive a social
  image and favicons from. Prefer reusing an existing high-res PNG/SVG over inventing art.
- **Page inventory:** list every indexable page so per-page metadata, the sitemap,
  and llms.txt are complete.
- **Language(s)** and whether the site is internationalized.

## 2. Implement the full checklist

### A. Per-page `<head>` (every indexable page, unique per page)
- Correct `<html lang="…">`, `<meta charset>`, and responsive `<meta name="viewport">`.
- Unique, descriptive `<title>` including the site/brand name.
- Unique `<meta name="description">` (~150–160 chars) summarizing that page.
- Absolute `<link rel="canonical" href="…">`.
- `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow, max-image-preview:large, max-snippet:-1, max-video-preview:-1">` (add a `googlebot` variant).
- `<meta name="author" content="…">`.
- `<meta name="theme-color">` (provide light/dark variants via `media`).

### B. Open Graph (LinkedIn / Facebook / Slack / Discord unfurls)
`og:type`, `og:site_name`, `og:locale`, `og:title`, `og:description`, absolute
`og:url`, absolute `og:image` (1200×630 PNG/JPG), plus `og:image:type`,
`og:image:width`, `og:image:height`, and `og:image:alt`.

### C. Twitter / X cards
`twitter:card=summary_large_image`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`,
absolute `twitter:image`, `twitter:image:alt`. Add `twitter:site`/`twitter:creator`
**only if** a real handle is known — never invent one.

### D. Structured data — JSON-LD (schema.org)
- Home: `WebSite` (+ `SearchAction` only if the site has real on-site search) and a
  `Organization` or `Person` as publisher/author.
- Content pages: the fitting type (`Article`/`TechArticle`/`BlogPosting`/`Product`/
  `Book`/`FAQPage`/`HowTo`/…), plus `BreadcrumbList` for inner pages.
- Cross-link entities with stable `@id`s (e.g. `#website`, `#author`).
- Emit as `<script type="application/ld+json">`; escape `<`, `>`, `&` so the JSON can't
  break out of the element; **validate that every block parses as JSON**.

### E. Favicons & app icons (place in the shipped output dir)
- `favicon.svg` (scalable — the primary icon for modern browsers).
- `favicon.ico` (16/32/48) as legacy fallback.
- `apple-touch-icon.png` (180×180, full-bleed square, **no transparency** — iOS masks corners).
- PWA icons `192×192` and `512×512` PNG, plus a **maskable** 512 with safe padding.
- Wire up: `<link rel="icon" href="favicon.ico" sizes="32x32">`,
  `<link rel="icon" href="favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml">`,
  `<link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="apple-touch-icon.png">`.
- If icons must be rasterized and the CI/build environment lacks image libraries,
  **pre-generate and commit** the raster files rather than adding a heavy build-time
  dependency; keep the generator script alongside for reproducibility.

### F. Web app manifest (`site.webmanifest`)
`name`, `short_name`, `description`, `id`, `start_url`, `scope`, `display`,
`background_color`, `theme_color`, `lang`, `dir`, `categories`, and an `icons` array
(include the maskable icon). Link it: `<link rel="manifest" href="site.webmanifest">`.

### G. Crawl directives & sitemap (site root of the shipped output)
- `robots.txt`: allow crawling, and reference the sitemap with an absolute
  `Sitemap:` line.
- **AI/agent crawler policy — ask me which you want, default = welcome:** add explicit
  groups for the major agents (e.g. `GPTBot`, `OAI-SearchBot`, `ChatGPT-User`,
  `ClaudeBot`, `Claude-User`, `anthropic-ai`, `PerplexityBot`, `Perplexity-User`,
  `Google-Extended`, `Applebot-Extended`, `CCBot`, `cohere-ai`, `Amazonbot`,
  `meta-externalagent`) with `Allow: /` (or `Disallow: /` if I choose to block them).
- `sitemap.xml`: every canonical URL with `<lastmod>` (and optional `changefreq`/
  `priority`). Keep it in sync with the page inventory.

### H. AI / LLM agent discovery
- `/llms.txt` (llmstxt.org format): an H1 title, a `>` blockquote summary, a short
  prose intro, then H2 sections of Markdown links (`[name](absolute-url): description`)
  covering the key pages plus authoritative external references.
- Consider `/llms-full.txt` with the full readable text of the site concatenated,
  when the content is worth exposing in full to agents.
- **Server-render for agents:** crawlers and most agents don't execute JS. If the site
  is a client-rendered SPA, flag it and ensure critical content + metadata are present
  in the initial HTML (SSR/prerender), otherwise the above has limited effect.

### I. Social/share image
Ensure a 1200×630 image exists and is reachable at the referenced absolute URL inside
the shipped output. Derive it from existing brand assets; add descriptive alt text.

### J. Crawlability hygiene
Absolute URLs in canonical/OG/JSON-LD; exactly one `<h1>` per page; semantic headings;
descriptive image `alt`; meaningful link text; HTTPS; consistent www/non-www and
trailing-slash behavior; a helpful 404 page; valid, mobile-friendly HTML. Add
`hreflang` if the site is internationalized. Preserve any existing custom-domain
config (e.g. a `CNAME` file).

### K. Optional extras (only with real data)
`humans.txt`; `/.well-known/security.txt` (only with a genuine contact); an RSS/Atom
feed for blogs; `preconnect`/`preload` hints for performance.

## 3. Verify before finishing
- Every JSON-LD block parses as valid JSON and uses correct schema.org types.
- `sitemap.xml` is well-formed XML; `site.webmanifest` is valid JSON.
- Every referenced asset (icons, social image, manifest, robots, sitemap, llms.txt)
  actually exists at its URL/path in the shipped output.
- Titles, descriptions, canonical and `og:url` are unique and correct per page.
- Re-run the site's build (if generated) and confirm the metadata is present in the
  output. Report a short summary of what was added and where.

## Constraints
- Never invent URLs, social handles, emails, or security contacts — detect them or ask.
- Prefer editing shared templates/generators over generated files.
- Don't degrade existing performance, accessibility, or deployment configuration.
